title: Versioned releases + changelog + outcome-tracking of deployments
context: For open-pictograms to sustain beyond the initial production push, it must have a versioned release process and a lightweight outcome-tracking system. Versioned releases (using the Elyos changeset convention) allow downstream consumers to pin to a known-good version; a changelog records what changed and why. Outcome-tracking records which partners are using which symbols in which deployments, keeping 'delivered' impact visible after the active development phase ends.
objective: Implement versioned releases (using the Elyos changeset/semver convention), a CHANGELOG, and a lightweight outcome-tracking record for deployments; integrate into the CI build.
acceptanceCriteria: Versioned releases use the Elyos changeset convention (changeset for user-facing changes); release tags are created in CI; each release includes a complete changelog entry listing added/changed/removed symbols and pipeline changes.
CHANGELOG.md is maintained and updated by CI on release; format is human-readable and machine-parseable.

Outcome-tracking record (docs/deployments.md or equivalent) lists confirmed deployments: partner name/type, deployment context, symbol set version, date, and (if available) beneficiary-validation notes — kept current as new deployments are confirmed.
Release process is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md or docs/release-process.md so maintainer rotation can follow it without oral briefing.
No secrets or PII in release artifacts or the CI release workflow; DCO sign-off enforced; licensed MIT (pipeline code).
output: CHANGELOG.md, docs/release-process.md, docs/deployments.md, and CI release workflow in .github/workflows/; licensed MIT. PR opened against main with CI running green.
